title: Level 981
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 981, "Fragmented Reality"}}__notoc__ {{Background|file=Screenshot 2026-01-13 062306.png}} {{CSS|content= /* Page */ .page { border: solid 2px rgb(0, 0, 0); margin-top: 12px; position: relative; border-radius: 5px; box-shadow: 0px 0px 8px 0 rgb(107, 110, 122); } /* Darkmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-dark { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(100, 100, 100, 0.8); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); --theme-link-color: #5F62FF; --theme-border-color: blue; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px brown; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px rgb(14, 65, 166); } --theme-community-header-color: white; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgba(14, 65, 166, 0.8); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: white; } /* Lightmode CSS */ .theme-fandomdesktop-light { --theme-page-background-color: rgba(100, 100, 100, 0.8); --theme-page-background-color--secondary: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); --theme-link-color: #5F62FF; --theme-border-color: blue; .page-side-tool { border: solid 2px brown; box-shadow: 0px 0px 5px rgb(14, 65, 166); } --theme-community-header-color: white; --theme-sticky-nav-background-color: rgba(14, 65, 166, 0.8); --theme-sticky-nav-text-color: #FFFFFF; --theme-sticky-nav-text-color--hover: white; }}} {{Class Variable|class=Variable|safety=Varying Safety|security=Varying Security|entity=Spatial and Gravitational Hazards}} ==Description== {{Blurimage|file=Screenshot 2026-01-13 062306.png|caption=An outside landscape in Level 981}} Level 981, also known as "Fragmented Reality", is a set of randomly assorted rooms and areas mimicking levels in the Backrooms. Areas of the level share similar, though unique, themes with each other, changing drastically over long distances (2-5 kilometers). These sublevels bend and contort randomly over long distances in a non-Euclidean fashion. Spaces within Level 981 are gravitationally unstable, while they remain at a constant 9.81 m/s^2This is the gravity of Earth, gravity bends to the floors akin to air, creating gravitational "currents" when floors intersect. {{Blurimage|file=Screenshot 2026-01-14 021403.png|align=left|caption=A floater inside a sublevel resembling [[Level 8]]}} [[The Void|No-clipping]] is unstable, with it sometimes leading one toward other sublevels instead of outside the level. It's theorized by wanderers that gravitational spikes may be the cause of these "internal no-clips," though this is unconfirmed. These sublevels can be similarly accessed by other common means of entering levels. The level's geometry, unlike [[Phenomenon 14]], Level 981's geometrical states vary no matter how much the level is observed or damaged. The level's outskirts are unpredictable, with many sections having fallen victim to [[The Void]] and [[The Broken]], and the intrinsic geometry at these outskirts resembles fractals, with paths splitting for infinity. No-clipping and gravitational hazards are equally extreme, with wanderers being tossed around spaces with little to no control over their movements. At a certain point, the level's gravitational hazards make it impossible to traverse any furtherExtreme gravitational pull in the level forms a small Roche limit, the distance where anything below that is ripped apart. It is theorized that the edge of the level transitions into either The Void or The Broken. The level has the unusual property of generating "floaters," which appear to be translucent, metaphysical objects that can only be pushed around. Floaters range in size and shape. Most are small and spherical, resembling metal; some floaters are larger in size, harboring their own gravity and akin to the rest of the level (though unable to be moved). Sublevels are theorized to be composed of floaters, although this is unconfirmed. Floaters create no-clip spots when they move through surfaces in the level, because of this, wanderers use them to traverse the level easier. Floaters of certain styles can induce memories in wanderers, usually ones deeper down in a person's psyche. Similarly, noclipping into a newer noclip spot by one of these floaters also triggers this effect. However, the effects of floater memories are still being studied today. ==Theories== Due to the mysterious nature of Level 981, multiple theories have emerged regarding its origin. {{Blurimage|file=The Broken Theory.png|caption=Fig. 2: Diagram of The Broken Theory|400px}} ===Theory 1: The Broken Theory=== The oldest theory about Level 981, The Broken Theory, postulates that Level 981 is one of the closest levels toward The Broken, and the outskirts is a "middle ground" between the two. Evidence supporting this theory is the fact that entrances into The Broken are more and more common as wanderers go further and further in, as well as the scattered, messy nature of the level, catching things that were meant to go into The Broken. Because it does not explain the fractal nature of the outskirts and the no-clipping, it is considered false and useless; it was overridden when the Midpoint Theory was made and the outskirts discovered. Today, fragments from this theory are used to form deeper, more sophisticated models of Level 981. Invented in 1954 by Karl H. Haevelsh after witnessing the entrances to The Broken inside the level. ===Theory 2: The Midpoint Theory=== {{Blurimage|file=Screenshot 2026-01-14 074648.png|caption=Fig. 2: Diagram of The Midpoint Theory|align=left|400px}} The midpoint theory suggests that level 981 is a midpoint between Backrooms levels, The Void, and The Broken. The level acts like a net, catching elements from all 3, taking the randomness from The Broken, the Themes from the Backrooms, and the entrances into the void, acting as where they intersect. The fractal nature is due to the themes gathering closer and closer to the "center". Evidence for this theory is how each theme resembles a level, and how people can enter most levels from Level 981. Invented by M.E.G in 1996 from studying the nature of the level, it has become one of the most popular theories, with many researchers trying to expand upon it in greater detail. ===Theory 3: Relations with [[The Kauer Thesis]]=== This theory suggests that the memories of Levels are dumped into Level 981, creating the mess of the Level. The outskirts act as older memories, being full of different paths and ways to go. Similarly, the geometry is manifested as many "interpretations" of where something could go, representing the thoughts of wanderers. Due to its alignment with every aspect of Level 981, it is the most popular and accepted theory. Invented by The Kauer Research Organization. ==Entities== Entities in Level 981 act similarly to how entities act in the backrooms, staying in places with similar themes to their native levels. However, anomalous behavior is observed in many entities, theorized to be them adapting to the nature of the Level. * Facelings: [[Entity 9|Facelings]] act closer to humans in Level 981, forming communities similar to wanderers. Along with this, they are at the bottom of the level's "food chain", with entities hunting them en masse due to the lack of humans. This is believed to have forced them to adapt because of predatory pressure and to act in groups like humans. Another anomalous property is a lower population of true facelings, with studies from their communities showing only 30% of facelings are true facelings. Again, presumed to be the cause of environmental pressure, causing them to evolve more sensory features to understand their surroundings. * Windows: All specimens of [[Entity 2]] in Level 981 have evolved a form of photosynthesis, similar to plants, with their windows showing large empty spaces painted black, or colored curtains and doors coated in chlorophyll. Currently, it is believed that they evolved this strategy because of the lack of humans, who would be motivated to look at them. ==Colonies and Outposts== Because Level 981 can mimic habitable levels, many colonies have been set up in the level. === Meg Base Zeta === * Founded by [[M.E.G.]] * Studies the level * Open to trade * Helps lead people out of the Level * 30-50 people at any given time === Kauer Research Base Manifestation === * Founded by the [[The Kauer Research Organization]] * Studies the level with the mindset of [[The Kauer Thesis]] * Not open for trade * 10-20 people at any given time === Haevelsh === * Founded by Karl E. Haevelsh in 1983 * Only human-founded settlement recorded * 700 people (940 including Facelings) * Open for trade and new members == Entrances and Exits == Many unstable entrances exist because of the Level's environment. This is a list of stable ones. === Entrances === * No-Clipping into physically impossible structures in [[Level 369]]. * Walking into areas with increasing gravitational anomalies in [[Level 67]] will lead one here. * Going into increasingly unstable areas at usually safe levels will lead one here. === Exits === * No-clipping in sublevels resembling other levels has a chance of no-clipping into that level. * Using other methods of entering levels in Level 981 can lead one to said level. * Going further and further out into the level, then no-clipping can lead to [[The Broken]] or [[The Void]]. {{Footnotes}} ... ... ...

{| class="fandom-table" |'''From:''' Unknown (████████████) (May Be Spam) [[File:Default Profile Picture.png|right|33x33px]] |- |'''To:''' [[M.E.G.]] Operative {{USERNAME}} ({{USERNAME}}@meg.bri) [[File:Default Profile Picture.png|right|33x33px]] |- |'''Subject:''' Look at what lies beyond {{USERNAME}} |} ---- {{Collapse|num=1|initial=Paradise.mp3, Open?|final=|body= -y *I've always been fascinated with "dreamy" landscapes. As a native backrooms child, my home of [[Level 399]] was comforting, and in my adult days, that has not faded. When I heard about this level, I came as soon as I could, clipping into a funny spot with a "heavy" feeling. I roamed and roamed, finding exotic sublevels on the way. Over my journey, I found a place resembling my home level 399, just as safe too! I was with my buddy Jerimiah and some others, so we decided to set up camp. *Oh, how much has changed since then. First, some faceling travelers came and set up near the camp, then some more people came, and now *chuckles* it's a whole community! We have around 150 to 160 people now? Anyways, jokes aside, some guys found something strange. We found a noclip spot in a hidden room anyway, my buddies and I are gonna go over there. *... *Welp, alright, we're over here in this place, wait, where is everyone? Hmmm, let's see, it looks just like a continuation of the 399 sublevel, don't know why I'm not somewhere else. Hey, wait a minute, this looks familiar, like the room of the Rostain hotel I used to live in, odd. Imma look around for a bit *exits room* Oh, they even have the... exact room number. Well, that's strange. Do I remember? Do I know? Hmm. *Hey, that looks like the section of the arcade I used to play in. Might play a few since some seem to be working. *... *Ya know, when I was young, 14-15, my parents and I were attacked by a skin stealer. Don't remember the details, apparently I was in [[Level 5]]. I wish they would come back... Hang on, why am I speaking about this? Before I came here, my therapist said those memories were so traumatic that they were stuck deep down in my memories, unable to be retrieved. *No-clips again* *Wha-where am I? This looks familiar... It's so dark. Oh, hey, one of my teammates! Hey! *The skin stealer unravels and starts to pursue the man* OH MY GOD *Pulls out gun and shoots the skin stealer, the man seems to notice something in the distance, even though there's nothing there* *Talking to nothing* Mom, Dad? It's been so long, where were you? I've been waiting, yes, I'll come with you, yes. I see... I see the light now. I must go, goodbye. *The camera drops* *End Log*}}
